analyst_observations: The with_skill version achieved 100% pass rate vs 97.2% without. The failure was on PascalCase schema naming — the without_skill baseline used lowercase schema names (notification, notificationPage) instead of PascalCase (Notification, NotificationPage). The skill's explicit naming convention table prevented this.

The with_skill audit was faster (197s vs 294s) and used fewer tokens (111k vs 131k) while producing a comparable number of findings (67 vs 34). The skill provided a structured checklist that allowed the agent to audit more systematically without spending time discovering what to check.

The build pipeline explanation was a near-draw — both versions correctly explained x-go-type and x-go-type-import. The with_skill version additionally explained the static import mapping layer in openapi.config.yml, providing a more complete picture. The without_skill version compensated by reading the actual build scripts to discover the mechanism independently.

The biggest efficiency gain is in the audit task, where the skill cut token usage by 15% and time by 33%. This makes sense — the audit checklist in the skill removes the need to discover audit criteria from first principles.
